data.title: Dominari Holdings announces entry into U.S. AI data center market
data.description:
• Dominari Holdings (NASDAQ:DOMH) has announced the creation of American Data Centers, whose members include Dominari, Donald J. Trump Jr., Eric Trump and other industry professionals in the AI space.
  • This new venture is designed to address the growing demand for high-performance computing infrastructure. Dominari currently holds a 32% interest in ADC.
  • On February 11, the Company announced that its board of directors had declared a special cash dividend of $4M, in the aggregate, to its stockholders of record as of the close of business on February 24, 2025.
  • Following the recent closing of Dominari's financings, it has been determined that the special cash dividend will be $0.32/share, ~$4.6M in the aggregate, and is expected to be paid on March 3, 2025.
  • DOMH -17.6% to $9.83.

data.title: Cemtrex's Vicon subsidiary secures $1.2M order for border protection deployment in Texas
data.description:
• Vicon Industries (OTC:VCON), a subsidiary of Cemtrex (NASDAQ:CETX) said on Tuesday that it has secured a significant new order valued at approximately $1.2M from an existing customer.
  • The order includes Vicon cameras, servers, and additional Valerus VMS licenses for deployment in a Texas border protection application.
  • With fulfillment expected in fiscal Q2 2025, the order adds to the company’s accelerating momentum, positioning Vicon to meet or exceed its growth targets for the year.
  • Cemtrex (CETX) shares were up more than 2.3% in Tuesday trade.

data.title: BASF downgraded at Berenberg on valuation, natural gas prices
data.description:
BASF (OTCQX:BASFY) (OTCQX:BFFAF) on Monday was downgraded to Hold from a previous investment rating of Buy among analysts at financial-services firm Berenberg. They said the chemicals maker is more fairly valued after rallying about 22% since early January.

“BASF trades above mid-cycle enterprise value to earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization,” Sebastian Bray, analyst at Berenberg, said in a February 17 report. “We do not see upside to consensus EBITDA for 2025/26, especially given current highs in spot European natural gas prices. This is not to mention the lurking threat of tariffs.”

data.title: Windtree Therapeutics approves 1-for-50 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Windtree Therapeutics (NASDAQ:WINT) has approved a 1-for-50 reverse stock split of the company’s common stock.
  • The reverse stock split will become effective on February 20, 2025, and WINT’s common stock is expected to begin trading on a reverse stock split-adjusted basis on Nasdaq on February 21, 2025.
  • The reverse stock split is intended to increase the price per share of the company’s common stock to allow the company to demonstrate compliance with the $1 minimum bid price requirement for continued listing on Nasdaq.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Toppoint expanding into refrigeration logistics
data.description:
• Toppoint Holdings (NYSE:TOPP) has announced its expansion into refrigeration logistics, marking a key milestone in its diversification strategy.
  • Through a new strategic collaboration with a leading refrigerated logistics provider, specializing in the safe and efficient transport of temperature-sensitive goods.
  • Toppoint will manage an increasing supply of refrigerated containers at the ports of Newark, NJ, Philadelphia, PA, and Baltimore, MD.
  • This expansion enhances Toppoint’s operational resilience by securing a consistent pipeline of work, mitigating the volatility caused by fluctuations in ship volume and port traffic.
  • By entering the refrigeration logistics sector, Toppoint aims to establish more stable revenue streams while enhancing its service offerings for both new and existing clients.

data.title: HF Foods Group secures revolving credit agreement of up to $125 million
data.description:
• HF Foods Group (NASDAQ:HFFG) on Tuesday said it has amended its credit agreement with JPMorgan Chase Bank and Comerica Bank to add Wells Fargo as an additional lender, and increased the company's aggregate revolving commitment by $25 million to $125 million.
  • “This additional revolver capacity will help support our growth strategy and the continued execution of our strategic transformation plan by providing us with further financial flexibility,” said Felix Lin, President and CEO of HF Foods.
  • HFFG -4.83% premarket to $2.36.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Lichen China receives Nasdaq delisting notice
data.description:
• Lichen China (NASDAQ:LICN) received a letter from Nasdaq stating that it is not in compliance with Nasdaq listing rule due to its securities having a closing bid price of $0.10 or less for eleven consecutive trading days from January 28 to February 11, 2025.
  • The letter indicated that Nasdaq has determined to delist the company's ordinary shares from Nasdaq.
  • The company was provided 180 calendar days, or until July 22, 2025, to regain compliance.
  • The company was provided until February 19, 2025, to request an appeal of the delisting determination to the hearing panel.
  • LICN intends to request such hearing to appeal the delisting determination before that date.
  • If the company fails to request an appeal of the delisting determination by February 19, 2025, trading of the company's ordinary shares will be suspended at the opening of business on February 21, 2025.
  • The company is considering all potential options available to it to regain compliance with the aforementioned rules, including seeking shareholders' approval for a reverse stock split.
  • LICN shares fall over 10% on Tuesday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Infinera expects sale to Nokia to close Feb. 28
data.description:
Infinera (NASDAQ:INFN) said its planned sale to Nokia (NOK) is set to be completed on or about Feb. 28 subject to gaining outstanding regulatory approvals.

Infinera (NASDAQ:INFN) also announced that the deadline to revoke a previously made election with respect to the form of merger consideration to be received in the deal is 5:00 p.m. EST on Friday, according to a statement on Tuesday.

The news comes after Reuters reported Friday that Nokia's planned $2.3 billion acquisition of Infinera (INFA) is set to be approved by Europe's antitrust regulator. The European Commission is expected to clear the transaction without conditions.

The deal was approved in the US in September after the HSR waiting period for the acquisition expired. Taiwan's antitrust regulator approved the Infinera (INFA) purchase last Wednesday.

data.title: Sohu.com outlines Q1 2025 guidance with brand advertising revenues of $30M-$40M
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Sohu.com Limited (NASDAQ:SOHU) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Dr. Charles Zhang, Chairman and CEO, highlighted that Sohu's Q4 brand advertising revenues met the high end of prior guidance, while online game revenues and bottom-line performance exceeded expectations. Efforts to refine products, optimize algorithms, and control budgets were emphasized as key drivers behind the company's performance.
  • Online games delivered solid results due to high-quality new game launches and revitalized legacy games. The TLBB series, including PC and mobile versions, performed well with expansion packs and new gameplay features driving higher user satisfaction.
  • Chairman Zhang announced that Sohu had repurchased 4.2 million ADS for an aggregated cost of $52 million as part of the ongoing share repurchase program.
  • CFO Joanna Lv detailed that quarterly revenues for the Sohu Media platform were $24 million, and quarterly operating loss was $69 million. Changyou's online game business reported quarterly revenues of $111 million with an operating profit of $48 million.

Outlook

  • For Q1 2025, brand advertising revenues are projected between $30 million and $40 million, representing an annual decrease of 13% to 19% and a sequential decrease of 26% to 31%.
  • Online game revenues are expected to range from $105 million to $115 million, indicating an annual decrease of 2% to 11% and a sequential change ranging from a 4% decrease to a 5% increase.
  • Non-GAAP net loss attributable to Sohu.com Limited is forecasted to be between $16 million and $26 million, while GAAP net loss is projected between $20 million and $30 million.

Financial Results

  • Q4 total revenues were $135 million, representing a year-over-year decrease of 5% and an 11% quarter-over-quarter decline. Brand advertising revenues reached $19 million, down 7% year-over-year and 1% quarter-over-quarter.
  • Online game revenues amounted to $110 million, reflecting a 4% year-over-year decline and a 14% sequential decrease.
  • GAAP net loss attributable to Sohu.com Limited was $21 million, compared to losses of $13 million in Q4 2023 and $16 million in Q3 2024. Non-GAAP net loss stood at $15 million, compared to $11 million in Q4 2023 and $12 million in Q3 2024.

Q&A

  • Thomas Chong, Jefferies: Asked about the weaker Q1 guidance for brand advertising and online games. Dr. Charles Zhang explained that macroeconomic factors, including reduced consumer spending, were the primary drivers of lower advertising budgets. CFO Joanna Lv added that Q1 gaming revenues are expected to remain flat quarter-over-quarter.
  • Alicia Yap, Citi: Queried about the impact of AI technologies and the buyback program. Dr. Zhang noted plans to integrate AI into Sohu's media and social network products for content generation and user interaction. Regarding the buyback, he stated that the $150 million program would continue at a steady pace, with $52 million already spent.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious sentiment, focusing on the impact of macroeconomic challenges on advertising and gaming revenues. Questions reflected concerns over weak advertiser budgets and broad economic pressures.
  • Management maintained a neutral tone during prepared remarks, highlighting achievements in gaming and media while acknowledging economic headwinds. During the Q&A, the tone shifted slightly negative when addressing advertising softness and guidance.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts displayed heightened skepticism regarding the guidance, while management's tone remained consistent, emphasizing long-term strategies and operational improvements.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for Q1 2025 reflects a significant sequential decline in brand advertising revenues, contrasting with Q4's performance, where advertising met high-end targets.
  • Online game revenues in Q4 declined 14% sequentially but remained a key contributor to overall revenues. Q1 guidance suggests stability with potential for slight growth.
  • Analysts in Q4 appeared more focused on macroeconomic impacts and AI integration, while Q3 discussions emphasized gaming performance and monetization opportunities.
  • Management's confidence level remained steady, emphasizing their commitment to product innovation and market competitiveness despite economic challenges.

Risks and Concerns

  • Macroeconomic conditions, particularly reduced consumer spending, were highlighted as a key risk impacting advertiser budgets.
  • Management acknowledged the ongoing pressure on advertising revenues and emphasized cost management and product innovation to mitigate these challenges.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the effectiveness of the company's strategies in addressing weak ad sentiment and sustaining gaming revenue growth.

Final Takeaway

Sohu.com Limited reported mixed Q4 2024 results, with online game revenues and bottom-line performance exceeding expectations, while overall revenues declined year-over-year. Guidance for Q1 2025 reflects ongoing macroeconomic challenges, with significant sequential declines projected in advertising revenues. Management remains focused on leveraging AI technologies, expanding gaming content, and executing the share buyback program to drive long-term growth and shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: WillScot declares $0.07 dividend
data.description:
• WillScot (NASDAQ:WSC) declares $0.07/share quarterly dividend.
  • Forward yield 0.74%
  • Payable March 19; for shareholders of record March 5; ex-div March 5.
